summarization.a: The chart shows the etch rate of various materials (Al2O3, HfO2, AlNx, TiN, SiO2) against the activation step duration. The etch rate increases with time for all materials.

data_extraction.a:
| activation step duration, s | SiO2 etch rate, nm/cyc | Al2O3 etch rate, nm/cyc | AlN etch rate, nm/cyc | HfO2 etch rate, nm/cyc | TiN etch rate, nm/cyc |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
|0|-0.5|-0.5|-0.5|-0.5|-0.5|
|5|0.4|0.0 | 0.0 | 0.0 | 0.0 |
|10| 0.6 | 0.2 | 0.2 | 0.1 | 0.0 | 
|20|0.7| 0.3 | 0.3 | 0.15 | 0.05 |

a.question: For the etching of aluminum based material, does it matter which type of material it is (i.e. nitride/oxide)?
a.answer: No it does not seem to matter. Both Al2O3 and AlNx have similar etch results.

a.question: Based on these results is it possible to have selective ALE, if so, which material can be selectively etched?
a.answer: Yes it is possible, SiO2 can be selectively etched wrt to all other materials at an activation time of 5 seconds.

a.question: Based on this data, can an ALE recipe be developed for TiN?
a.answer: Yes, there is an increase in etch rate for increasing activation time

a.question: Which activation step durations should be applied to have similar etching of HfO2 and Al2O3?
a.answer: 20 s for HfO2 and 10 s for Al2O3.
